source.description: One reviewed six-panel marker contact sheet cropped into five process masters and a final sun master.
source.notes: Keep the same round face-free sun, sixteen alternating rays, oversized black sunglasses, short lower smile, and small teal shadow in every panel.

process_strategy: The six-frame marker sheet grows one circular summer sun into a ray pattern, oversized sunglasses, and a simple mouth before marker color; the final only reinforces established marks and fills.

final_elements.name: sun circle
final_elements.description: One large centered circular sun body.

final_elements.name: alternating rays
final_elements.description: Sixteen short alternating triangular and straight rays around the circle, with orange accents on the triangular rays.

final_elements.name: sunglasses frames
final_elements.description: Two oversized tilted black lenses linked by a bridge and arms.

final_elements.name: lower smile
final_elements.description: A short relaxed curved mouth below the established glasses.

final_elements.name: yellow sun fill and teal shadow
final_elements.description: Marker color applied to the existing sun circle and a small shadow under it.

frames.visible_job: Place one large light circular sun body in the center.
Add a balanced ring of alternating rays and orange accents around the established sun circle.
Draw and black-fill two oversized sunglasses lenses, bridge, and short arms across the existing circle.
Place one short relaxed curved mouth below the established sunglasses.
Fill the existing sun circle yellow and add a small teal shadow below.
introduces: yellow sun fill and teal shadow

final_step.visible_job: Only thicken existing outlines, tidy existing yellow, orange, teal, and black marker fills, and add tiny white highlight gaps to the established sunglasses.

rejection_checks: Reject if the sun circle, ray count, glasses, or mouth changes between panels.
Reject if a ray, glasses part, mouth, or color is first introduced in the final panel.
Reject a default two-dot-eye face; the eyes must remain hidden by large sunglasses and the expression relies on the simple mouth.
